title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds architecture and responsibility decision record, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of architecture and responsibility decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Filesystem Object Cdn
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 5272 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Filesystem Object Cdn.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and cdns by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Filesystem Object Cdn, keep Filesystem Object Cdn connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 5272 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Filesystem Object Cdn happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
State the Filesystem Object Cdn preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
S3 is a mounted POSIX filesystem with CDN behavior included. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 1129 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

Inspect the Cost Failure Model trace for this workshop case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Origin Path Selection · changed case · Workshop Origin Path Selection
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 7796 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Cost Failure Model happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Cost Failure Model condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
